smart_moves: Centralizing or eliminating lingering `WIP` markers helps make repository status less ambiguous for future contributors.
The PR appears intentionally narrow, which is appropriate for mechanical cleanup of project-wide wording or status markers.
decomp_lessons: Avoid leaving broad `WIP` labels in checked-in decompilation code unless they communicate actionable, current information.
When status markers are removed, prefer precise labels such as TODOs tied to concrete matching, naming, typing, or documentation work.
Small cleanup PRs can reduce search noise for future decomp work, especially when contributors rely on grep-able markers.
naming_conventions: The PR title implies `WIP` should not remain as a persistent repository marker, but the replacement convention is not visible in the available evidence.
